title: When an effect is needed deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Proving that an effect is unnecessary requires design review of alternate render/event placement; a passing UI cannot establish absence of needless synchronization.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: When an effect is needed: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Any calculation after state changes belongs in useEffect.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Calculate render-derived values during render; use effects only to synchronize with systems outside React.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Calculate render-derived values during render; use effects only to synchronize with systems outside React.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Calculate render-derived values during render; use effects only to synchronize with systems outside React.
Any calculation after state changes belongs in useEffect.

answer.explanation: Calculate render-derived values during render; use effects only to synchronize with systems outside React.
